extracted_information: Kubernetes NetworkPolicies enable control over traffic flow at the IP address or port level (OSI layer 3 or 4) within a cluster and between Pods and the outside world, specifically for TCP, UDP, and SCTP protocols. They are an application-centric construct defining how a pod communicates with various network entities. Policies are additive and do not conflict; allowed connections are the union of applicable policies. For a connection to be allowed, both the source pod's egress policy and the destination pod's ingress policy must permit it. NetworkPolicies require a network plugin that supports their enforcement to have any effect.

specifications.policy_definition_layer: OSI Layer 3 (IP address) and Layer 4 (port level)
specifications.unspecified_protocols_behavior: Undefined for protocols like ARP or ICMP; may vary across network plugins for both allow and deny rules.
identifiable_entities: IP blocks (traffic to/from node where Pod runs is always allowed)

policy_application_logic.default_behavior: By default, pods are non-isolated for both ingress and egress (all traffic allowed) if no NetworkPolicies apply to them.
policy_application_logic.isolation_trigger: A pod becomes isolated for a direction (ingress/egress) if any NetworkPolicy selects it and includes that direction in its `policyTypes`.
policy_application_logic.allowed_connections_logic: When isolated, only connections explicitly allowed by the `ingress` or `egress` lists of applicable NetworkPolicies are permitted. Reply traffic for allowed connections is implicitly allowed.
policy_application_logic.additive_nature: Effects of `ingress` and `egress` lists combine additively. Policies are additive; the union of what applicable policies allow determines permitted traffic.
policy_application_logic.conflict_resolution: Network policies do not conflict; order of evaluation does not affect the result.

podSelector.description: Selects the grouping of pods to which the policy applies. An empty `podSelector` selects all pods in the namespace.

policyTypes.description: List indicating whether the policy applies to `Ingress`, `Egress`, or both. Default `Ingress` if ingress rules exist, `Egress` if egress rules exist, when `policyTypes` is unspecified.

ingress.description: List of allowed inbound rules. Each rule defines traffic matching `from` and `ports` sections.

egress.description: List of allowed outbound rules. Each rule defines traffic matching `to` and `ports` sections.

selectors_behavior.podSelector_in_rules: Selects particular Pods in the same namespace as the NetworkPolicy.
selectors_behavior.namespaceSelector_in_rules: Selects particular namespaces (all Pods within them).
selectors_behavior.namespaceSelector_and_podSelector_combin: Selects particular Pods within particular namespaces. (YAML syntax requires careful nesting).

ipBlock_in_rules.description: Selects IP CIDR ranges. Primarily for cluster-external IPs, as Pod IPs are ephemeral. Behavior with network address rewriting (LoadBalancer, node IP) is implementation-defined and can vary.
ipBlock_in_rules.cidr_exception: Use of `except` for specific IP ranges within a CIDR block.

constraints: `endPort` can only be defined if `port` is also defined.

features: Traffic control at IP address or port level (OSI layer 3/4) for TCP, UDP, SCTP.
Application-centric definition of network communication rules.
Isolation of pods for ingress and egress traffic independently.
Additive policy evaluation, allowing union of rules.
Selection of target pods using label selectors (`podSelector`).
Definition of allowed traffic sources/destinations via `ipBlock` (CIDR ranges).
Definition of allowed traffic sources/destinations via `namespaceSelector` (labels).
Definition of allowed traffic sources/destinations via `podSelector` (labels).
Ability to define default policies for namespaces (deny all ingress, allow all ingress, deny all egress, allow all egress, deny all ingress and egress).
Support for targeting a range of ports using `endPort` field (v1.25+).
Ability to target multiple namespaces by label (`matchExpressions`).
Ability to target a namespace by its immutable `kubernetes.io/metadata.name` label.
Guaranteed filtering effectiveness throughout the Pod lifecycle, including init containers and sidecar containers.
Implicit allowance of reply traffic for allowed connections.
references: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-pod-configmap/ (Configure a Pod to Use a ConfigMap)
